title: Composing a Musical Touch (Change-Ringing Blueline)
strategies: trivial: the smallest possible valid touch (single out-and-back swap)
greedy: nearest-neighbor BFS visit of musical rows one at a time, ignoring the reflection structure -- can strand itself (fail to close true) when targets are spread out

strong: route only a HALF-length touch from rounds to the reverse row (the unique adjacency-preserving involution), then mirror the calls (index j -> n-j) to close automatically; a beam search chases musical rows valuing both a row and its automatic mirror image, so every hit near the pivot is worth double
invalid: garbage output that violates closure and trueness
